body.p: Every new version of comes with a lot of new features, improvements, bug fixes, and security upgrades. Therefore, you should always use the latest version. However, you may want to perform some internal tests before allowing the users to use the new features. For example, you may want to disable some of the new features to keep the UI simple or to tweak some of the settings.
It is recommended that you make a separate deployment of the new version on a different VM, perform some acceptance tests, and then switch all of your users to the new deployment.

p.note: configuration (options), custom plugins, and custom frameworks are stored in the data directory. If you install a new version without migrating the content of the previous data directory, the configuration will be reset to defaults and your plugins/frameworks will not be available in the new installation.

ol.li: Back up the data directory used by the old version.
Deploy the new version of on a separate virtual machine and connect it to your existing license server. The two deployments will work in parallel, both using the same license pool.

Copy the relevant content from the old data directory into the new data directory so that your environment is preserved (for example, global options, custom frameworks, and custom plugins).If you used options that are stored encrypted, you must also migrate the .java directory from the old data directory. Otherwise, encrypted values cannot be reused in the new installation. See the note about the .java directory in Web Author Oxygen Data Directory.

It might be necessary to update your license key. If you receive an error message regarding the license key version, follow this procedure. Your existing deployment will continue to work with a license for the newer version.

Review the settings in the Administration Page and confirm that your deployment-specific configuration is correctIf you did not migrate the old data directory, you will need to reconfigure these settings manually.
Switch all of the users to the new version. This can be achieved either by changing the DNS entry or configuring the Load Balancer to use the new deployment.
